purpose: Defines the `!update_dashboard [dashboardId]` command for generating a visual progress report of the current swarm. Reads initialization.json + all progress files; identifies most-recently-completed task by latest completed_at; computes swarm stats (completed/in_progress/failed/pending counts, pct, overall_status, elapsed, wave_summary). Renders a richly formatted markdown report: header with truncated prompt → Status/Project/Elapsed → Progress (text bar + counts) → Waves table → Latest Completed (with Milestones + Deviations) → Completed Tasks table → cross-swarm Deviations → Remaining tasks grouped by wave. Read-only — does not modify any files.
gotchas: Read-only — explicitly does NOT modify progress files or logs (despite the command name suggesting it might)
Progress bar width is 20 chars; mini bar is 10 chars; uses █ filled and ░ empty
Latest completed is determined by latest completed_at (not started_at, not progress file mtime)
Duration format depends on magnitude: <60s = `Ns`, 60-3600s = `Mm Ss`, >3600s = `Hh Mm`
Wave status logic: ✅done = all completed, 🔴failed = any failed, 🔵running = any in_progress, ⚪pending = all pending (failed status takes precedence)
Summary text in Completed Tasks table is truncated to 80 chars; prompt in header is truncated to 120 chars + …
patterns: 6-step procedure: parse → read init → read progress → identify latest completed → compute stats → render report
Report sections in fixed order: header → status line → Progress bar → Waves table → Latest Completed → Completed Tasks → Deviations Across Swarm → Remaining (grouped by wave)
Progress bar formula: filled = round((completed/total)*20); bar = '█'.repeat(filled) + '░'.repeat(20-filled)
Conditional sections (only render if data present): Latest Completed milestones, Latest Completed deviations, Deviations Across Swarm, Remaining
conventions: Format reference subsections (Progress Bar, Mini Bar, Status Emojis, Wave Status, Duration) at the bottom
Markdown render template uses {placeholder} interpolation syntax with comments inside braces explaining computed values
